assertions.description: window.DeviceOrientationEvent exists — the orientation event interface the static requestPermission() hangs off.
window.DeviceMotionEvent exists — the motion event interface the companion static requestPermission() hangs off.
DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission is a function — a STATIC member on the interface object (not on event instances), per the spec IDL.
DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission is a function — the companion STATIC member on its interface object, taking no parameter.
The event interfaces are [SecureContext]: on a non-secure context DeviceOrientationEvent must be absent (on a secure context either exposure state is spec-legal).
When the gate is present, requestPermission lives only on the interface object: an event instance constructed from DeviceOrientationEvent does not carry its own requestPermission property.
assertions.test: (()=>{ const DOE = window.DeviceOrientationEvent; if (!DOE || typeof DOE.requestPermission !== 'function') return true; return !('requestPermission' in new DOE('conformance-probe')); })()

assertions.description: Both statics accept zero required arguments — orientation's only parameter (absolute, default false) is optional and motion takes none — so function.length is 0 on both (checked without invoking them: no permission is requested).
assertions.test: (()=>{ const DOE = window.DeviceOrientationEvent, DME = window.DeviceMotionEvent; if (!DOE || typeof DOE.requestPermission !== 'function') return false; if (!DME || typeof DME.requestPermission !== 'function') return false; return DOE.requestPermission.length === 0 && DME.requestPermission.length === 0; })()
